title: Vriesea arpocalyx
taxonomy: Vriesea arpocalyx is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. The Bromeliaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as pineapples or air plants. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Vriesea arpocalyx is classified under the subfamily Tillandsioideae. Tillandsioideae is one of the most ancient and diverse subfamilies within the Bromeliaceae family. Vriesea arpocalyx is specifically a genus of air plants within the Tillandsioideae subfamily.

intro: The Vriesea arpocalyx, also known as the "Furry Tillandsia," is a type of epiphytic bromeliad plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Vriesea arpocalyx include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vriesea arpocalyx include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
